reviewer: weekly-hardening-routine (headless Chromium 141 via CDP; no chrome-devtools-mcp in cloud env)

spec_match.rationale: Uses fieldset checkable=single/multiple with defaultchecked exactly as the explainer shows, and reads real element checked state when upgraded.

interactive.rationale: Click-through verified in both configurations; radio semantics worked flagged (150% -> true, others false).

edge_cases.rationale: Flagged run surfaced that this Chromium build does not initialise checked from defaultchecked — the page displays the real values honestly, but does not yet call out that build gap explicitly.

conformance.rationale: menuitem-checked-member assertion in the feature suite.

runtime_evidence.rationale: Dual-config CDP runs with screenshots; real vs fallback readouts both exercised.

telemetry_health.rationale: State-read assert wired; KV not queried.

design_system.rationale: CSS variables; check marks shown via ::before plus text state, not colour alone.

accessibility.rationale: Readout is text-based and live; menuitemradio/menuitemcheckbox role mapping not captured from the accessibility tree in the flagged run.

summary: Checkable groups work; the page reports real checked values including current implementation gaps.

openQuestions.title: Call out the defaultchecked initialisation gap
openQuestions.detail: In the tested Chromium 141 build behind MenuElements, defaultchecked did not initialise .checked=true. Add a short observed-behaviour note (and re-test on current Canary) so visitors are not confused by unchecked defaults.
